title: OACP Error Payload (v1)
description: Payload schema for messages with payloadType = 'error-v1'. Carries a structured error result emitted when message processing fails at any of the twelve processing gates. Follows the error-result schema's shape; this v1 schema makes the payloadType binding explicit. Per spec §1.2 (gate ordering) and the per-section error code registries.

errorCode.description: Reserved error code from the relevant section's registry. Examples: MESSAGE_TENANT_VIOLATION, BINDING_INVALID, PROPOSAL_INVALID, HASH_CHAIN_BROKEN, SELF_DECISION_VIOLATION, EXTENSION_WEAKENS_CORE, RESPONSE_CORRELATION_FAILED.

errorMessage.description: Human-readable message. MUST NOT leak tenant data or sensitive payload content. Recipients MUST treat this as advisory.

gateStep.description: Which step of the §1.2 processing gate produced the error. Step 1.5 is encoded as 1.5.

violatingField.description: Optional JSON pointer to the envelope field that triggered the error (e.g., '/scope/tenantId', '/causation/causationId').

messageRef.description: Reference to the message that triggered the error.

tenantId.description: Tenant scope of the error. SYSTEM-scope errors carry the receiver's authorized tenant context.

details.description: Additional structured details. Shape is error-code-specific. Implementations MUST NOT include sensitive content here.

retryable.description: Hint to the sender about whether retry could succeed. False for hard rejections (tenant violation, self-decision); true for transient failures (downstream timeout). Advisory only; not authoritative.
